items.title: HTML becomes editable Word documents
items.oneliner: Node library converts semantic HTML into native DOCX files with tables, lists, images, and visual regression tests.

items.title: Kernel firewall matches Layer 7 traffic
items.oneliner: eBPF-based L7 firewall moves HTTP policy checks into the kernel, cutting userspace overhead to nanosecond-scale decisions.

items.title: Token overhead benchmark for coding agents
items.oneliner: Wire-level comparison shows Claude Code sends 33k tokens before prompts, versus 7k for OpenCode.

items.title: Git users can learn Jujutsu fast
items.oneliner: Hands-on tutorial teaches Jujutsu workflows, conflicts, and core concepts for developers switching from Git.

items.title: Query any web table with PRQL
items.oneliner: Browser extension lets you run PRQL against HTML tables, making Wikipedia-scale pages filterable without copy-pasting to CSV.

items.title: A tiny branch quadruples performance
items.oneliner: Deep performance walkthrough shows how one seemingly useless if unlocked 4x faster compression code.

items.title: Accessibility review in 128 questions
items.oneliner: Practical WCAG 2.2 study guide covers ARIA, semantic HTML, keyboard access, and contrast with plain-English answers.

items.title: A first PCB from sensor to board
items.oneliner: Build log walks through designing a BME280 breakout in KiCad, from datasheets to a manufacturable PCB.

items.title: Tiny emulators for learning 8-bit systems
items.oneliner: Open-source emulator set teaches 8-bit architecture through runnable code, assembly experiments, and compact documentation.

items.title: Procedural UI sounds without audio files
items.oneliner: This synthesis tool generates lightweight UI sounds in code, replacing bundled audio assets with procedural effects.
